description: Oracle Database monitoring template via the ORACLE subagent. Monitors every Oracle database connection configured in the agent: global metrics use instance discovery against the Oracle.Connections list, so one set of DCIs is created automatically per configured database Id (no hard-coded Id). Collects instance health (reachability, status, archiver, log mode), critical stats (offline objects, failed jobs, deadlocks, invalid objects, datafiles needing recovery), performance counters (sessions, cache hit ratio, locks, logical/physical I/O), and per-tablespace usage. Per-tablespace DCIs use a script-based instance discovery (Oracle::TablespaceInstances) that enumerates every tablespace of every configured database, so tablespaces are covered for all connections, not just the first. Alarm lifecycle is self-contained: every threshold raises a template-owned ORA_* event and clears via ORA_THRESHOLD_CLEARED through dedicated create and terminate EPP rules keyed on ORA_THRESHOLD_*, so alarms close correctly on fresh and upgraded servers without relying on the built-in threshold rules. The archiver check fires only for ARCHIVELOG databases. Two companion templates add OS-level process monitoring that auto-binds by platform: Oracle Process Monitoring (Linux) tracks the ora_* background processes with a PMON-based service-down check (no memory-percentage threshold, since the shared SGA makes per-process RSS sums misleading), and Oracle Process Monitoring (Windows) tracks the single oracle.exe process with an accurate memory-usage threshold. Includes 14 Oracle-specific events plus a shared clear event, create/terminate EPP rules, and three object tools (Sessions, Tablespaces, Data Files) that prompt for the database Id. Auto-binds to nodes whose agent has the ORACLE subagent loaded.

events.name: ORA_INSTANCE_UNREACHABLE
events.description:
Generated when Oracle.DBInfo.IsReachable stops returning YES, indicating the agent cannot connect to the configured Oracle instance.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue

events.name: ORA_INSTANCE_NOT_OPEN
events.description:
Generated when Oracle.Instance.Status is not OPEN. Possible values include STARTED, MOUNTED, OPEN, OPEN MIGRATE - anything other than OPEN means the database is not serving sessions.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue

events.name: ORA_ARCHIVER_STOPPED
events.description: Generated when the archiver process is not STARTED (STOPPED or FAILED) while the database runs in ARCHIVELOG mode. Archiving failure will hang the database once redo log groups fill - act immediately. Does not fire for NOARCHIVELOG databases.
events.message: Oracle archiver process is not running (status: %<currentValue>)

events.name: ORA_TABLESPACE_FULL_WARN
events.description: Generated when a tablespace exceeds the warning usage threshold (default 85%). Plan to add datafiles or enable autoextend. Override per node or per template via the custom attribute Threshold.OracleTablespaceUsedWarning.

events.name: ORA_TABLESPACE_FULL_CRIT
events.description: Generated when a tablespace exceeds the critical usage threshold (default 95%). New allocations will fail soon; the database may stop accepting writes for that tablespace. Override per node or per template via the custom attribute Threshold.OracleTablespaceUsedCritical.

events.name: ORA_DATAFILE_RECOVERY_NEEDED
events.description:
Generated when one or more datafiles need media recovery. Indicates block corruption or media failure - restore from backup and apply archive logs.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue

events.name: ORA_OFFLINE_OBJECTS
events.description:
Generated when offline datafiles, tablespaces, or non-ONLINE tablespace status is detected. Investigate via Oracle Tablespaces and Data Files object tools.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue, instance

events.name: ORA_SHUTDOWN_PENDING
events.description: Generated when Oracle.Instance.ShutdownPending returns YES, meaning a database shutdown has been requested and is in progress.

events.name: ORA_FAILED_JOBS
events.description: Generated when one or more Oracle jobs have a non-zero failure count.

events.name: ORA_SEGMENTS_CANNOT_EXTEND
events.description: Generated when one or more segments have reached their maximum extents and cannot grow further.

events.name: ORA_DEADLOCKS
events.description: Generated when the database registers one or more deadlocks per minute.

events.name: ORA_CACHE_HIT_LOW
events.description: Generated when the data buffer cache hit ratio (averaged over five samples) falls below the configured threshold, indicating possible memory pressure. The threshold defaults to 90%. Set the custom attribute Threshold.OracleLowCacheHitRatio on the node (or on the template, to apply to every bound node) to override the percentage without editing the template.
events.message: Oracle: data buffer cache hit ratio low (%<currentValue>%%)

events.name: ORA_THRESHOLD_CLEARED
events.description: Generated when an Oracle metric returns to its normal range. Terminates the alarm raised by the corresponding Oracle threshold event.

events.name: ORA_SERVICE_DOWN
events.description: Generated when no Oracle OS process is detected on the host (no ora_pmon_<SID> on Linux, no oracle.exe on Windows), indicating the instance is down or has crashed.
events.message: Oracle OS process is not running

events.name: ORA_PROCESS_MEMORY_HIGH
events.description: Generated when the oracle.exe process consumes more system RAM than the configured threshold (Windows only). Review SGA/PGA sizing and the overall workload.
events.message: Oracle process memory usage high: %<currentValue>%% of system RAM (threshold %<thresholdValue>%%)

templates.name: Oracle Server
templates.comments:
Oracle Database monitoring template (ORACLE subagent).

Auto-binds to nodes whose agent currently has the ORACLE subagent
loaded. Global DCIs use instance discovery against the Oracle.Connections
list and produce one DCI per configured [oracle/connections/<id>]
section, regardless of the connection Id - no Id is hard-coded, so the
template works as-is for any database name and for several databases on
one agent.

==== Oracle user setup =========================================

Create a monitoring user with read-only access to system views:

  CREATE USER netxms IDENTIFIED BY <password>;
  GRANT CREATE SESSION TO netxms;
  GRANT SELECT_CATALOG_ROLE TO netxms;

SELECT_CATALOG_ROLE covers all v$/dba_* views the subagent queries
(v$instance, v$database, dba_tablespaces, dba_data_files,
v$session, dba_objects, v$sysstat, etc.).

==== Agent configuration =======================================

SubAgent = oracle.nsm

[oracle/connections/ORCL]
Endpoint = //db-host:1521/ORCLPDB1
Login    = netxms
Password = <password>

The connection Id is the subsection name (ORCL above); Oracle.Connections
reports it and the template discovers it automatically. Configure
additional [oracle/connections/<id>] sections to monitor more databases
on the same agent - each gets its own set of global and tablespace DCIs.

==== Tablespace coverage =======================================

Per-tablespace DCIs are created by the script-based instance discovery
Oracle::TablespaceInstances, which walks Oracle.Connections and, for each
database, Oracle.TableSpaces(<id>), returning one instance per
(database, tablespace) pair. The DCI parameter is keyed on
"<id>,<tablespace>" and the description shows "<tablespace>@<id>", so
every tablespace of every configured database is covered, including
system tablespaces (SYSTEM, SYSAUX, UNDOTBS1, TEMP).

SYSTEM and SYSAUX are expected to grow toward their max sizes; if they
generate noisy 85%% warnings, raise the threshold on the bound node or
exclude those tablespaces by disabling the discovered DCI.

dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: database version

dcis.name: Oracle.DBInfo.OpenMode({instance})

dcis.name: Oracle.CriticalStats.DFOffCount({instance})

dcis.name: Oracle.CriticalStats.TSOffCount({instance})

dcis.name: Oracle.CriticalStats.FullSegmentsCount({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: number of segments that cannot extend

dcis.name: Oracle.CriticalStats.Deadlocks({instance})

dcis.name: Oracle.Objects.InvalidCount({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: number of invalid objects

dcis.name: Oracle.Sessions.Count({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: number of open sessions

dcis.name: Oracle.Cursors.Count({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: number of open cursors

dcis.name: Oracle.Cursors.MaxPerSession({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: max open cursors per session

dcis.name: Oracle.Performance.FreeSharedPool({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: free space in shared pool

dcis.name: Oracle.Performance.CacheHitRatio({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: data buffer cache hit ratio

dcis.name: Oracle.Performance.Locks({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: number of locks

dcis.name: Oracle.Performance.LogicalReads({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: logical reads per minute

dcis.name: Oracle.Performance.PhysicalReads({instance})
dcis.description: Oracle [{instance}]: physical reads per minute

dcis.name: Oracle.Performance.PhysicalWrites({instance})

dcis.description: Oracle tablespace {instance-name}: percentage used

dcis.description: Oracle tablespace {instance-name}: status

dcis.description: Oracle tablespace {instance-name}: bytes used

templates.name: Oracle Process Monitoring (Linux)
templates.comments:
OS-level monitoring of Oracle background processes on Linux nodes.

Auto-binds to Linux nodes whose agent currently sees at least one ora_pmon_<SID> process (the instance monitor). Tracks aggregate CPU time, threads, open file descriptors and page faults across all ora_* processes, plus resident/virtual size.

Service-down detection: Process.CountEx(^ora_pmon_) fires ORA_SERVICE_DOWN when no instance (PMON) process is seen for two consecutive samples.

No memory-percentage threshold is shipped for Linux on purpose: Oracle's background processes all map the shared SGA, so summing per-process RSS counts the SGA once per process and grossly overstates real memory use. RSS/VMSize are collected for reference only. Use SGA/PGA metrics in the Oracle Server template for real memory monitoring.

dcis.name: Process.CountEx(^ora_pmon_)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: number of running instances (PMON processes)

dcis.name: Process.RSS(^ora_,sum,.*)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: total resident set size (includes shared SGA, counted per process)

dcis.name: Process.VMSize(^ora_,sum,.*)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: total virtual memory size

dcis.name: Process.CPUTime(^ora_,sum,.*)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: CPU time consumed per minute

dcis.name: Process.Threads(^ora_,sum,.*)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: total number of threads across all processes

dcis.name: Process.Handles(^ora_,sum,.*)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: total open file descriptors

dcis.name: Process.PageFaults(^ora_,sum,.*)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: page faults per minute

templates.name: Oracle Process Monitoring (Windows)
templates.comments:
OS-level monitoring of the Oracle instance process on Windows nodes.

Auto-binds to Windows nodes whose agent currently sees an oracle.exe process. On Windows the whole instance runs as a single oracle.exe process, so memory and CPU are measured accurately.

Service-down detection: Process.Count(oracle.exe) fires ORA_SERVICE_DOWN when the process disappears for two consecutive samples.

Memory threshold: Process.MemoryUsage(oracle.exe,sum) warns above 80%% of system RAM averaged over three samples. Override per node or per template via the custom attribute Threshold.OracleProcessHighMemoryUsage without editing the template.

dcis.name: Process.Count(oracle.exe)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: number of running oracle.exe processes

dcis.name: Process.MemoryUsage(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: memory usage as percentage of system RAM

dcis.name: Process.RSS(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: total resident set size (working set)

dcis.name: Process.VMSize(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: total virtual memory size

dcis.name: Process.CPUTime(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: CPU time consumed per minute

dcis.name: Process.Threads(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: total number of threads across all processes

dcis.name: Process.Handles(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: total open handles

dcis.name: Process.PageFaults(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: page faults per minute

dcis.name: Process.IO.ReadB(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: bytes read from disk per minute

dcis.name: Process.IO.WriteB(oracle.exe,sum)
dcis.description: Oracle processes: bytes written to disk per minute

rules.comments: Raise an alarm when any Oracle template threshold is reached. Alarm severity follows the event severity so notifications and actions see the same level.
Terminate the alarm when the Oracle threshold returns to normal.

scripts.name: Oracle::TablespaceInstances

objectTools.name: &Oracle->&Tablespaces
objectTools.description: Show all tablespaces with size and usage (dba_tablespaces / dba_data_files).

objectTools.name: &Oracle->&Data files
objectTools.description: Show all Oracle datafiles with status, size, and I/O statistics.
